title: althaea speciosa
intro: Althaea speciosa, also known as the western or California marshmallow, is a perennial herb belonging to the mallow family. Its roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating digestive issues, sore throats, and skin irritations. The medicinal properties of Althaea speciosa include its ability to calm irritated mucous membranes, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing, leading to various health benefits such as improved respiratory function, reduced inflammation, and enhanced skin health. Althaea speciosa is often prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, or used as a topical cream to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
